timeline.description: The Election Commission of India officially announced the schedule for the 2026 State Legislative Assembly Elections.
details: Announcement Date: 15 March 2026
Model Code of Conduct came into effect immediately
States Covered: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, Puducherry
Government cannot announce new policies or schemes from this date
Election Commission sets up monitoring and flying squads

timeline.description: Five states and one union territory successfully conducted the 2026 Assembly Elections.
details: West Bengal — 294 constituencies (2 phases)
Tamil Nadu — 234 constituencies (single phase)
Kerala — 140 constituencies (single phase)
Assam — 126 constituencies (single phase)
Puducherry — 30 constituencies (single phase)

timeline.description: First phase of polling across Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry was successfully completed.
details: Kerala — 9 April 2026 (All 140 seats)
Assam — 9 April 2026 (All 126 seats)
Puducherry — 9 April 2026 (All 30 seats)
High voter turnout recorded across all regions
Peaceful polling reported with minor incidents addressed
EVMs and VVPATs functioned efficiently

timeline.description: Polling in Tamil Nadu and the first phase of West Bengal was successfully completed.
details: Tamil Nadu — 23 April 2026 (All 234 seats)
West Bengal — 23 April 2026 (Phase 1)
Voter turnout exceeded expectations in urban centers
Special arrangements for elderly and disabled voters were utilized
Strict adherence to security protocols ensured a smooth process

timeline.description: Second and final phase of polling in West Bengal was successfully concluded.
details: West Bengal — 29 April 2026 (Phase 2)
Final constituencies covered under tight security
Central Armed Police Forces ensured order at sensitive booths
Webcasting used for real-time monitoring

timeline.description: Repolling was conducted in select booths in West Bengal as ordered by ECI.
details: Repolling Date: 2 May 2026
Necessary to ensure fair representation and address complaints
Conducted under heightened vigilance and observation
Results integrated with main counting data

timeline.description: Counting of votes was completed across all designated centers on May 4, 2026.
details: Counting Date: 4 May 2026
Transparent process witnessed by candidates' agents
Postal ballots and EVM rounds processed efficiently
Random VVPAT verification matched the EVM records

timeline.description: Final results have been declared for all five states, and the 2026 Assembly Elections are now officially concluded.
details: All winners officially notified by the Election Commission
Election results gazetted and published in full
Government formation process initiated in West Bengal, TN, Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry
Electoral rolls to be updated for future local body polls

guides.title: Voter Registration
guides.description: Official process to register as a voter and obtain your EPIC card

steps.title: Check Eligibility
steps.content: Indian citizens aged 18 or above on the qualifying date are eligible for enrollment.

steps.title: Gather Documents
steps.content: Prepare valid proof of age, address, and recent passport-size photographs.

steps.title: Fill Form 6
steps.content: Submit Form 6 online via voters.eci.gov.in or the official Voter Helpline App.

steps.title: Submit Application
steps.content: Complete the submission online or at your local Electoral Registration Office.

steps.title: Verification Visit
steps.content: The Booth Level Officer will visit your address to verify provided details.

steps.title: Receive Voter ID
steps.content: Once approved, your name is added to the roll and your EPIC card is issued.

guides.title: How to Vote
guides.description: The standardized procedure for casting your ballot on election day

steps.title: Find Your Polling Station
steps.content: Locate your assigned station using the ECI portal or Voter Helpline App.

steps.title: Carry Valid ID
steps.content: Present your EPIC card or one of the 12 approved alternative photo IDs.

steps.title: Arrive at Polling Station
steps.content: Visit during polling hours and follow the queue management system.

steps.title: Identity Verification
steps.content: Officials will verify your details and apply indelible ink to your finger.

steps.title: Cast Your Vote
steps.content: Press the blue button on the EVM for your candidate and verify via VVPAT.

steps.title: Exit Process
steps.content: After voting, exit the station. Your democratic contribution is complete.

guides.title: Postal Ballots
guides.description: Voting procedure for eligible citizens via the postal service

steps.title: Check Eligibility
steps.content: Available for service voters, officials on duty, and specified senior citizens.

steps.title: Apply for Postal Ballot
steps.content: Submit Form 12 to the Returning Officer within the designated timeline.

steps.title: Receive Ballot Paper
steps.content: The ballot paper and instructions will be sent to your registered address.

steps.title: Mark Your Vote
steps.content: Mark your choice in privacy and sign the mandatory declaration form.

steps.title: Return the Ballot
steps.content: Send the sealed ballot back via post to reach before counting starts.

faqs.question: What is the minimum voting age in India?
faqs.answer: The minimum voting age in India is 18 years. You must be at least 18 years old on the qualifying date (usually January 1st of the year the electoral roll is prepared).

faqs.question: What is NOTA?
faqs.answer: NOTA stands for 'None Of The Above'. It is an option on the Electronic Voting Machine (EVM) that allows voters to officially register a rejection of all candidates contesting in their constituency.

faqs.question: Is the 2026 election concluded?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 2026 Assembly Elections for West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry concluded with the counting of votes on May 4, 2026.

faqs.question: How do I check my name in the voter list?
faqs.answer: You can check your name in the electoral roll online at the ECI Voter Portal (voters.eci.gov.in) or by using the Voter Helpline App. You can search by details or by your EPIC number.

faqs.question: What is an EPIC card?
faqs.answer: EPIC stands for Electors Photo Identity Card. It is the official identity document issued by the Election Commission of India to all eligible voters to facilitate voting and prevent electoral fraud.

faqs.question: Can I vote without a Voter ID card?
faqs.answer: Yes, if your name is in the electoral roll, you can vote by showing any of the 12 alternative photo identity documents approved by the ECI, such as Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Driving License, or Passport.

faqs.question: How can I register to vote?
faqs.answer: New voters can register by filling Form 6 online on the Voter Portal or Voter Helpline App. You need to provide proof of age, proof of residence, and a photograph.

faqs.question: What is VVPAT?
faqs.answer: Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPAT) is an independent system attached to the EVM that allows voters to verify that their vote was cast correctly. It prints a paper slip showing the candidate's name and symbol for 7 seconds.

faqs.question: What are 'Service Voters'?
faqs.answer: Service voters are individuals serving in the Armed Forces, Central Armed Police Forces, or government employees posted outside India. They can cast their votes via postal ballots.

faqs.question: How do I find my polling station?
faqs.answer: You can find your assigned polling booth on your Voter Slip, via the Voter Helpline App, or by using the 'Polling Booth Finder' tool on this portal which uses your current location.

faqs.question: Can NRIs vote in Indian elections?
faqs.answer: Yes, Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) can register as overseas electors. However, they must be physically present at their respective polling station in India to cast their vote.

faqs.question: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
faqs.answer: The Model Code of Conduct (MCC) is a set of guidelines issued by the ECI to political parties and candidates during elections, ensuring free and fair polling by regulating speeches, polling day conduct, and government announcements.

faqs.question: What is a 'Tendered Vote'?
faqs.answer: If a voter finds that someone else has already voted in their name, they can approach the Presiding Officer. After verification, they are allowed to cast a 'Tendered Vote' on a ballot paper.

faqs.question: What is the maximum limit for election expenditure?
faqs.answer: The ECI sets expenditure limits for candidates, which varies for Parliamentary and Assembly constituencies. Candidates are required to maintain a daily account of all election-related expenses.

faqs.question: How can I report a violation of election rules?
faqs.answer: Citizens can use the 'cVIGIL' app by the Election Commission to report any violations of the Model Code of Conduct or electoral malpractices in real-time with photo/video evidence.

glossary.definition: Electors Photo Identity Card - the official voter ID card
Electronic Voting Machine used for casting votes
Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail for vote verification
Election Commission of India - the constitutional body conducting elections
